summary: Matched a small set of previously stubbed Yoshi special-move functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c and tightened one header prototype. The PR replaced placeholder markers for ftYs_SpecialHi_Coll, ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04, and ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Coll with C implementations using existing fighter helper routines and corrected ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04 from an unknown signature to void ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
what_changed: Implemented ftYs_SpecialHi_Coll by calling ft_8008403C with the Yoshi gobj cast to Fighter_GObj* and callback fn_8012E3B4 cast to void (*)(ftYs_GObj*).
Implemented ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04 as a Fighter_GObj* function that calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with motion state 0x170, flags 0x0C4C5088U, and three external ftYs_Init float values.
Added extern declarations in the C file for ftYs_Init_804D9A5C, ftYs_Init_804D9A60, and ftYs_Init_804D9A64 to support the matched Fighter_ChangeMotionState call.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Coll by checking ft_80082708((Fighter_GObj*) gobj) against GA_Ground and entering fall with ftCo_Fall_Enter(gobj) when the condition is met.
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h so ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04 has the concrete prototype void ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04(Fighter_GObj* gobj) instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Reused existing common fighter collision/transition helpers instead of expanding logic inline, which is typical for small matching wins in fighter special-state code.
Preserved required type casts between ftYs_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, and HSD_GObj-style APIs, indicating attention to both matching and current project typedef boundaries.
Converted an unknown header prototype only after the implementation established a concrete parameter and return type.

For ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04, matched the action-state transition through the canonical Fighter_ChangeMotionState API with explicit state ID, flags, animation start, frame rate, blend, and NULL argument.
decomp_lessons: Small collision-state stubs often collapse to wrapper calls around generic fighter helpers such as ft_8008403C, ft_80082708, ft_80084EEC, and ft_80084F3C.
When replacing a placeholder function, update the matching header prototype at the same time to remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and reduce future callsite ambiguity.
Yoshi special move code may require local externs for data symbols such as ftYs_Init_804D9A5C, ftYs_Init_804D9A60, and ftYs_Init_804D9A64 when those values feed Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Dummy local padding such as u8 _[8] can be a matching tactic for stack layout in short collision callbacks; here it appears in ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Coll.
Ground/air transition callbacks can be matched by checking helper return values against GA_Ground and dispatching to a common fall-enter routine.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter special functions use the ftYs_ prefix and encode move/state variants such as SpecialHi, SpecialAirHi, SpecialLwLanding, and SpecialS in the symbol name.
Unidentified Yoshi-specific data symbols retain address-based names under ftYs_Init_804D9A5C-style naming.
Unresolved internal callbacks may remain address-style names such as fn_8012E3B4 until their purpose is better understood.
Header comments preserve the function address prefix, for example /* 12EA04 */ beside ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used function-pointer casting in ftYs_SpecialHi_Coll to satisfy the expected helper signature while preserving the known Yoshi callback type.
Used explicit unsigned hex flags, 0x0C4C5088U, in Fighter_ChangeMotionState, likely to match constant typing/codegen.
Introduced a local u8 _[8] stack dummy in ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Coll, likely to force stack allocation or instruction shape for matching.
Kept object-pointer casts explicit at helper boundaries: ftYs_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* for fighter helpers.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but no human_text_excerpt was provided, so no commenter intent or requested changes can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or type fn_8012E3B4.
Search for other ftYs_Init_804D9A5C/804D9A60/804D9A64 usages to centralize declarations or improve symbol names.
Compare nearby Yoshi special collision functions for the same ft_8008403C and ft_80082708 transition patterns.
Check whether the stack dummy in ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Coll was later removed or replaced by a cleaner matching construct.
